theme_notes: FIFA World Cup 2026 opens in four days across USA/Canada/Mexico (48 teams, $0 ticketing glitches, water-bottle drama); Pope Leo XIV is mid-tour in Spain for the first papal visit in 15 years; Trump's administration proposed fresh forced-labor tariffs on 60 countries, averaging a $1,500 hit per US household.
settled: All square. FIFA couldn't balance a spreadsheet this clean.
Zero balance. Like a World Cup 0-0 draw — nobody wins, but you're technically fine.
The Pope flew to Spain to perform miracles. Zero owed might actually count as one.
youOwe: You owe less than a World Cup stadium hot dog. Disgraceful, but manageable.
FIFA sold tickets for $0 by accident. Your debt has no such excuse.
Trump tariffed 60 countries. You can't clear $40. Think about that.
That's a World Cup nosebleed seat. You're living in the nosebleed section of this relationship.
One month of Trump's 2026 tariff surcharge per household. You're levying their goodwill.
Pope Leo XIV flew from Rome to Madrid. Venmo takes three seconds. Use it.
You could've had World Cup tickets — real ones, not the $0 glitch ones. You chose debt.
That's three months of the average US household's tariff hike. Embarrassing company.
The Vatican governs a sovereign state on better margins than you right now.
You're approaching the average American's full 2026 tariff burden. They didn't choose it. You did.
FIFA is running 48 teams across three countries. You can't manage one tab.
The Pope's on a week-long European tour. Your debt's been on a longer one.
For this you could've bought World Cup quarterfinal seats. You chose liability instead.
Trump tariffed 60 countries. Every single one is paying more on time than you.
At this level, you qualify for a US forced-labor debt investigation.
This could fund a small nation's entire World Cup qualifying campaign. You funded nothing.
The 2026 tariff regime costs less per capita in most of those 60 countries. You ARE the deficit.
The Pope is blessing crowds in Madrid. You need an exorcism, not a payment plan.
theyOwe: They owe you less than a FIFA-approved stadium water bottle. Collect it anyway.
Under $50. The tariff on one imported widget costs more. Still — it's yours.
The Pope forgives sins. You don't have to.
They owe you a World Cup group stage ticket in cash. They didn't buy you one. Noted.
Trump's tariff on a single country costs more. Your partner is in structural violation.
The Vatican has better international collections than your Venmo request history.
That's two World Cup group stage tickets. They spent it on themselves. Obviously.
Three months of tariff burden per household. They're running a personal trade deficit.
The Pope came all the way to Spain to perform miracles. Getting paid would qualify.
That's World Cup good-seat money. They chose dishonor over attendance.
More than a US household's full 2026 tariff bill. Your partner IS the trade war.
The Vatican has better financial oversight than whatever is happening in their account.
For this you could've gone to a World Cup knockout match. You got a debtor instead.
Trump opened forced-labor probes for less per country. Consider yours opened.
Pope Leo is in Madrid blessing thousands. You're here: unblessed, unpaid, and patient.
This is FIFA World Cup hospitality package money. They chose chaos.
Trump tariffed 60 entire countries for less per citizen than this. File a Section 301.
At this point, call the Vatican. This isn't a debt. It's a holy war.
